steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: For coordinate-circle questions, read the center and radius from the equation or graph the circle as a check. For diagram facts, mark equal radii, diameters, tangents, and arcs before doing algebra.

steps.title: Identify the standard form of a circle equation
steps.content: The standard form of a circle equation reveals the center and radius directly. If an equation matches this pattern, the center is at $(h,k)$ and the radius is $r$, with no additional work needed.

steps.title: Match the given equation to the standard form
steps.content:
The given equation is $$(x-15)^2 + (y-k)^2 = 81$$ Comparing directly:
  • $(x-15)$ means $h = 15$
  • $(y-k)$ means the $y$-coordinate is $k$
  • $81 = 9^2$, so $r = 9$
The center is at $(15, k)$ and the radius is $9$. That is the correct answer.
